_comment: Machine-readable rules for tgk-audit. Source of truth: _docusign-template-spec.md. Edit this file when the spec changes.

banned_fonts.name: Playfair Display
banned_fonts.reason: Not a Docusign brand font — remove everywhere

banned_fonts.name: Playfair
banned_fonts.reason: Not a Docusign brand font — remove everywhere

known_vendor_point_solutions._comment: Named incumbents IAM does NOT try to displace. Grouped by industry for readability; audit uses the flat union.
public_sector: Oracle Public Sector Compliance and Regulation

process_nouns_rejected_as_point_solution._comment: If a point chip reads like a workflow step rather than a named vendor, it's a content bug. These tend to be 'identification' when the real vendor is Jumio, or 'underwriting' when the real vendor is the PAS / UW engine.

workspaces_coherence._comment: Rules that govern how 'Workspaces' should be narrated once it appears in a pipeline. Feeds the LLM-judgment Workspaces pass in SKILL.md.
expected_narrative_beats: Workspaces should be introduced at the step where cross-party collaboration or shared review begins (underwriting referrals, claims triage, case review, policy servicing).
Once introduced, subsequent future-state steps should reference the shared space OR cleanly hand off to another IAM product — not silently silo back into isolated tools.
Workspaces should sit alongside the parties it coordinates (agent + carrier, claimant + adjuster + legal, applicant + advisor + custodian) — never as a solo replacement for a single-actor task.
If Workspaces replaces an email-thread / SharePoint / shared-drive point solution in current.points, the future bullet should explicitly describe the shared space, not just 'routed through workflow'.

interface_parity._comment: Rules for comparing a current-state component mock to the future-state component mock it's paired with. Feeds the LLM-judgment Parity pass in SKILL.md.
checks: Same aspect ratio and canvas size (both mocks render inside the same modal iframe).

Same data fields present on both sides — if the vendor mock shows SSN last 4, DOB, address, the Docusign mock should show the same identifiers in the equivalent position (or explicitly absorb them into a single field with commentary).
Parallel information density — one mock shouldn't be a skeletal 'wireframe' while the other is a rich dashboard.
Vendor name in current-state matches the vendor actually cited in the paired current.points of the steps JSON.
Future-state mock title names the IAM product(s) listed in the paired future.tags — no silent product swap.
Friction chips on the vendor side should have a matching resolution / simplification on the Docusign side (not a mute).
Same swim-lane industry context (retail-banking vs insurance-pc vs hls etc.) — parity only makes sense within a lane.
